calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-20-2022 Continued To Jan-04-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Action
Off Calendar Jun-10-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BEVERAGES & MORE, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Action.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted.

Plaintiff's sole argument, that California Assembly Bill 51 (2019) makes the arbitration provision unenforceable, fails. AB 51 is preempted by the Federal Arbitration Act ("FAA"). (Cal. Lab. Code sec. 432.6(f) ["Nothing in this section is intended to invalidate a written arbitration agreement that is otherwise enforceable under the Federal Arbitration Act"]; Chamber of Com. of United States v. Bonta (9th Cir. 2021) 13 F.4th 766, 781 ["we hold that Government Code [section] 12953 and Labor Code [section] 433 are preempted to the extent that they apply to executed arbitration agreements covered by the FAA."].)

Defendant shows that the FAA applies to the parties' agreement because it involves interstate commerce. (Purves Decl., 2 ["BevMo owns and operates stores in California, Arizona, and Washington. BevMo's principal place of business is in Concord, California. The products that BevMo sells come from various places throughout the United States and even internationally. BevMo also does business through the BevMo.com website, whereby BevMo sells products to customers in all 50 states."]; see also Giuliano v. Inland Empire Pers., Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1276, 1286-88.)

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
